finding: Publish is gated by readiness blockers revealed one at a time in terse internal naming ("Fix SEO title" then "Fix content 1 · Cta Label" then "Fix content 1 · Destination URL"), so the full checklist of what to fix is never shown at once.

evidence.quote: draft · SEO Blocked; chip 'Fix SEO title'; Publish disabled
Publish is gated behind a chain of readiness blockers that are revealed ONE AT A TIME in the status chip, in terse internal naming

persona_rationale: As a first-timer I can't fix what I can't see; solving one blocker only to be shown the next feels like the app is hiding the ball and I'd consider giving up.

suggested_fix.current: Single rotating status chip showing one blocker at a time
suggested_fix.proposed: Complete plain-language checklist: 'Before publishing: 1. Add a hero headline 2. Add a CTA label 3. Add a CTA destination URL.'

finding: The publish chip says 'Fix SEO title' / 'Improve SEO title' while the SEO title field's own placeholder says 'Leave blank to use page headline' — a direct contradiction.

evidence.quote: chip 'Fix SEO title' shown beside field placeholder 'Leave blank to use page headline'
/placeholder: Leave blank to use page headline

persona_rationale: This contradiction destroyed my trust in the readiness guidance — if the app is wrong about an optional field, why believe any of its other warnings?

suggested_fix.current: Blocker labelled 'Fix SEO title' on a field marked 'Leave blank to use page headline'
suggested_fix.proposed: Name the real problem: 'Add the keyword "vending machine ux review" to your headline or SEO title'; never flag a field that says it is optional

finding: A required-to-publish field ('CTA destination URL') is not on the canvas — it exists only inside Block actions → Edit settings modal — yet the blocker 'Fix content 1 · Destination URL' gives no hint where to find it.

evidence.quote: 'CTA destination URL' field only present in Block settings modal
Hunted on canvas for 'Destination URL' — it is only in the block settings modal

persona_rationale: I spent real time hunting the canvas for a field that wasn't there; a first-timer with no mental model of 'block settings' would assume the app is broken.

suggested_fix.current: Blocker 'Fix content 1 · Destination URL' with no location hint
suggested_fix.proposed: Deep-link the blocker to the field: 'Add a CTA destination → Open block settings', matching the existing 'Open SEO settings' / 'Go to field' actions

finding: After confirming publish, the 'Changes published.' toast appears but the inline confirm card stays open asking to publish again, so it is unclear whether the publish succeeded.

evidence.quote: Confirm publish — This will make this draft visible at /resources/ux-seo-review-test-1781071765035.
'Changes published.' toast appears BUT the confirm card remains open asking to publish again

persona_rationale: Leaving the 'are you sure?' card open after success made me think it failed and almost click Confirm again.

suggested_fix.current: Confirm-publish card remains open after the success toast
suggested_fix.proposed: Close the confirm card on success and replace it with 'Published — View live page →'

finding: No 'View live page' / 'Open live page' link is offered after publishing in the default panel state — the public URL had to be typed manually.

evidence.quote: No 'Open live page' link was visible after publishing in the default panel state — the live URL had to be typed manually.

persona_rationale: The whole point of publishing is to see it live; making me reconstruct the URL by hand is a basic gap a first-timer wouldn't know how to work around.

suggested_fix.proposed: Surface a prominent 'View live page →' button linking to /resources/{slug} immediately after publish

finding: The READINESS and STATUS columns show only colored dots with no visible text label in the cell, so the meaning of each color is not discoverable without clicking.

evidence.quote: Readiness and Status columns rendered as colored dots only
button "SEO readiness: Needs work" / button "Page status: Published" (present only as ARIA, not visible cell text)

persona_rationale: Color alone is meaningless to a first-timer (and invisible to colour-blind users); I'd have to click each row to learn the page's state.

suggested_fix.proposed: Show a short text badge next to each dot ('Needs work', 'Published', 'Draft') or a visible legend

finding: No revision/undo exists while drafting; the empty state assumes internal terms and a draft mangled before first publish has no page-level undo.

evidence.quote: revisions do NOT exist while drafting. Empty state: 'Revisions appear after publishing, library refreshes, or draft restores.'

persona_rationale: As a nervous first-timer I expect an undo before I commit anything; learning there's none only after I've broken my draft is exactly the 'scary, irreversible' moment that makes me distrust the tool.

suggested_fix.current: Restore points only created on publish; empty state 'Revisions appear after publishing, library refreshes, or draft restores.'
suggested_fix.proposed: Create a restore point on each draft save and reword to 'Your saved versions will appear here. We save one each time you publish.'

finding: After filling a schedule date/time and saving, the field re-renders empty and no 'Scheduled for {date}' status appears anywhere, so the schedule cannot be confirmed.

evidence.quote: Scheduled publish field shows 'dd/mm/yyyy, --:-- --' after save
the field re-rendered EMPTY and no 'Scheduled for …' status appeared anywhere in the editor

persona_rationale: With no confirmation I genuinely don't know if my page will go live or not — a first-timer would either re-submit or assume it's broken.

suggested_fix.current: Schedule field clears on save with no confirmation
suggested_fix.proposed: Persist the value and show 'Scheduled to publish on 11 Jun 2026, 12:00 PM AEST'

finding: The schedule field is labelled 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)' on an Australian business's CMS.

evidence.quote: Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles). Leave blank unless this page should publish later.
Label says 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)' — for an Australian business

persona_rationale: I'd have to do timezone math to schedule correctly, and I have no reason to expect a Vendingpreneurs page to run on US West Coast time.

suggested_fix.proposed: Use the business local timezone (Australia/Adelaide) or show the admin's local time with an explicit label

finding: A new page canvas is entirely greyed placeholders ('Hero headline', 'Hero body copy', 'CTA label') with no indication which fields are mandatory before publishing.

evidence.quote: greyed placeholders 'Hero headline', 'Hero body copy', 'CTA label'
New page canvas is entirely placeholder ... with no guidance that these must be completed before publishing.

persona_rationale: I'd happily fill required fields up front if the app told me which they were, instead of being ambushed by blockers later.

suggested_fix.current: Placeholder-only canvas with no required markers
suggested_fix.proposed: Mark required-to-publish fields with a 'Required' tag or asterisk on the canvas

finding: Typing a page title then leaving the unsaved editor navigates straight back to the list with no unsaved-changes warning, silently losing the typed input from the editor flow.

evidence.quote: Leaving unsaved editor: navigated straight to /admin/pages with NO unsaved-changes warning — typed title silently lost
returned to pages list after leaving unsaved editor

persona_rationale: A first-timer clicking around to explore will lose work without warning, which is exactly the kind of silent loss that makes me abandon a tool.

suggested_fix.current: No prompt when leaving an unsaved editor
suggested_fix.proposed: Prompt 'You have unsaved changes — leave anyway?' before navigating away

finding: The redirects table has no edit or delete control, so a mistyped redirect is permanent from this UI.

evidence.quote: Redirects table has no delete/edit controls — created redirects cannot be removed from this UI.
redirects table rows with no per-row actions

persona_rationale: As a first-timer I make typos; a redirect I can't undo from the screen that created it feels unsafe and would make me afraid to use the feature at all.

suggested_fix.current: No row actions in redirects table
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Delete' (and ideally 'Edit') action per redirect row with a confirm step

finding: The validation error 'Path must be root-relative.' is jargon and doesn't tell me how to fix it.

evidence.quote: Path must be root-relative.

persona_rationale: 'Root-relative' is developer vocabulary; a first-timer marketer doesn't know what's wrong or what a valid value looks like.

finding: A developer-QA page ('Block preview audit') is reachable in the admin surface with no explanation of why a content editor would use it.

evidence.quote: Block preview audit — Compare every picker preview against the real resource-page render using mocked block content.

persona_rationale: Landing here as a first-timer makes me unsure whether this is part of my job; it adds doubt about what the product actually is.

suggested_fix.current: Block preview audit exposed in admin surface
suggested_fix.proposed: Hide behind a dev/internal flag, or label it 'Internal QA — block render parity'
